readme.text: # justoneapi —— 值得信赖的数据合作伙伴 >[官网](https://justoneapi.com?source=github&detail=justoneapi/data-api) | [接口文档](https://doc.justoneapi.com?source=github&detail=justoneapi/data-api) 我们是一家专业的数据服务提供商，提供标准的 HTTP 接口服务，并可根据您的需求定制化数据服务。 ## 联系方式 如有任何问题，欢迎联系我们。 [Contact](https://justoneapi.com/contact) ## 服务概览 以下是我们目前提供的各平台接口服务。此列表有时会更新不及时，可联系我们获取最新的在线实时接口文档，查看最新 API 列表。 ### 小红书 - 小红书笔记详情 - 小红书用户 - 小红书用户笔记列表 - 小红书笔记评论 - 小红书笔记子评论 - 小红书笔记搜索 - 小红书用户搜索 - 小红书主页Feed流 ### 抖音（视频） - 抖音视频详情 - 抖音用户详情 - 抖音用户视频列表 - 抖音搜索视频 - 抖音搜索用户 ### 快手 - 快手视频详情 - 快手用户详情 - 快手用户视频列表 - 快手搜索用户 ### 淘宝 - 淘宝商品详情(带优惠券信息，券后价，销量，sku信息) - 淘宝商品评论 - 淘宝店铺商品列表 - 淘宝商品销量（滚动30天精确销量） - 淘宝搜索商品 - 淘宝商品描述（商品底部描述图） ### 拼多多（可合作） - 商品详情 - 商品搜索 - 店铺商品列表 ### 京东外卖 - 按经纬度获取商家信息 ### 美团 - 美团商超药店 ### 抖音电商 - 抖音商品详情 ### 抖音（电商） - 抖音小黄车（小黄车直播间商品列表） - 抖音店铺商品列表 - 抖音商品评论 ### Temu - Temu商品详情 ### Lazada - Lazada商品详情 ### SHEIN - SHEIN商品详情 ### Shopee - Shopee商品详情 ### 京东 - 京东商品详情 ### 微信公众号 - 微信公众号用户发文 - 微信公众号文章阅读点赞 - 微信公众号文章评论 - 微信公众号搜索 ### 小红书蒲公英 - 蒲公英平台KOL各类数据 ### 抖音星图 - 蒲公英平台KOL各类数据 ### 腾讯广告互选 - 腾讯广告互选平台KOL各类数据 ### 大众点评 - 店铺基本信息 ### 微博 - 微博搜索 - 微博详情 ### 其他 - 哔哩哔哩 - 知乎 - Bigo - 贝壳 - 百度指数 - 携程 - Boss直聘 - 智联招聘 - 拉钩 - 今日头条 - Facebook - YouTube - Instagram - Twitter

repo_cards.description: Vite + Electron with esbuild, so fast! ⚡

readme.text:
# ⚡Vite + Electron & Doubleshot Template This template is used to build vite + electron projects. Build with [Doubleshot](https://github.com/Doubleshotjs/doubleshot), crazy fast! 🎉 [Doubleshot](https://github.com/Doubleshotjs/doubleshot) is a whole new set of tools to help you quickly build and start a node backend or electron main process. This template is based on a small framework [einf](https://github.com/ArcherGu/einf) that I wrote myself, which may not be complete, if you want to apply to production, you can use the templates with integrated nest.js: - [Vue.js template](https://github.com/ArcherGu/fast-vite-nestjs-electron) - [React template](https://github.com/ArcherGu/vite-react-nestjs-electron) - [Svelte.js template](https://github.com/ArcherGu/vite-svelte-nestjs-electron) ## Features - 🔨 [vite-plugin-doubleshot](https://github.com/archergu/doubleshot/tree/main/packages/plugin-vite#readme) to run/build electron main process.
- 😎 Controllers/Services ipc communication, powered by Typescript [decorators](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/decorators.html).
- ⚡ Rendering process is powered by [Vite](https://vite.io/).
- ⏩ Quick start and build, powered by [tsup](https://tsup.egoist.sh/) and [electron-builder](https://www.electron.build/) integrated in [@doubleshot/builder](https://github.com/Doubleshotjs/doubleshot/tree/main/packages/builder) ## Motivation In the past, I've been building desktop clients with [vue](https://v3.vuejs.org/) + [vue-cli-plugin-electron-builder](https://github.com/nklayman/vue-cli-plugin-electron-builder), and they work very well. But as the project volume grows, webpack-based build patterns become slower and slower. The advent of [vite](https://vitejs.dev/) and [esbuild](https://esbuild.github.io/) greatly improved the development experience and made me feel lightning fast ⚡. It took me a little time to extract this template and thank you for using it. ## How to use - Click the [Use this template](https://github.com/ArcherGu/fast-vite-electron/generate) button (you must be logged in) or just clone this repo. - In the project folder: ```bash # install dependencies pnpm install # run in developer mode pnpm dev # build pnpm build ``` ## Note for PNPM This project requires `pnpm` as the package manager. In order to use with `pnpm`, you'll need to adjust your `.npmrc` to use any one the following approaches in order for your dependencies to be bundled correctly (ref: [#6389](https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/6289#issuecomment-1042620422)): ``` node-linker=hoisted ``` ``` public-hoist-pattern=* ``` ``` shamefully-hoist=true ``` ## Relative My blog post: - [极速 DX Vite + Electron + esbuild](https://archergu.me/posts/vite-electron-esbuild) - [用装饰器给 Electron 提供一个基础 API 框架](https://archergu.me/posts/electron-decorators)

repo_cards.description: 🦋 Production-Ready AT Protocol & Bluesky SDK for Flutter & Dart 💙

readme.text:
AT Protocol and Bluesky Social Things for Dart/Flutter

--- [![Test/Analyzer](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/actions/workflows/test.yml) [![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/myConsciousness/atproto.dart?logo=open-source-initiative&logoColor=green)](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/blob/main/LICENSE) [![Contributor Covenant](https://img.shields.io/badge/Contributor%20Covenant-2.1-4baaaa.svg)](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) [![GitHub Sponsor](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Sponsor&message=%E2%9D%A4&logo=GitHub&color=ff69b4)](https://github.com/sponsors/myConsciousness) [![melos](https://img.shields.io/badge/maintained%20with-melos-f700ff.svg?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/invertase/melos) [![Reference](https://api.netlify.com/api/v1/badges/f088b033-6670-455e-baa2-1cbb9577ebaa/deploy-status)](https://atprotodart.com) --- - [1. About atproto.dart 💪](#1-about-atprotodart-) - [2. Package Ecosystem 📦](#2-package-ecosystem-) - [2.1. Core Libraries](#21-core-libraries) - [2.2. Client Libraries](#22-client-libraries) - [2.3. Utilities & Tools](#23-utilities--tools) - [2.4. CLI Tool](#24-cli-tool) - [2.5. GitHub Actions Workflow](#25-github-actions-workflow) - [3. Quick Start Guide 🚀](#3-quick-start-guide-) - [3.1. Building a Bluesky Social App](#31-building-a-bluesky-social-app) - [3.2. Building AT Protocol Services](#32-building-at-protocol-services) - [4. Project Development Setup 🛠️](#4-project-development-setup-️) - [4.1. Prerequisites](#41-prerequisites) - [4.2. Quick Setup](#42-quick-setup) - [4.3. Development Commands](#43-development-commands) - [4.4. Troubleshooting](#44-troubleshooting) - [4.5. Contributing](#45-contributing) - [5. Who is using atproto.dart? 👀](#5-who-is-using-atprotodart-) - [6. Support](#6-support) Welcome to **[atproto.dart](https://atprotodart.com)** 🦋 **The comprehensive Dart/Flutter SDK for the AT Protocol ecosystem** - empowering developers to build the next generation of decentralized social applications with Bluesky and beyond. ## 1. About atproto.dart 💪 The AT Protocol represents a paradigm shift toward decentralized social networking, and [Bluesky](https://blueskyweb.xyz) is leading this transformation. As the ecosystem grows rapidly with millions of users and thousands of developers building innovative applications, the need for robust, well-designed development tools has never been greater. **[atproto.dart](https://atprotodart.com)** is the most comprehensive and battle-tested SDK for AT Protocol development in Dart and Flutter. Whether you're building mobile apps, web applications, bots, or backend services, our SDK provides everything you need to integrate with the AT Protocol ecosystem efficiently and reliably. ### Why Choose atproto.dart? **For Mobile & Web Developers:** - Build cross-platform social apps with Flutter using production-ready AT Protocol integration - Access the complete Bluesky API including posts, feeds, profiles, messaging, and moderation - Leverage built-in OAuth DPoP authentication for secure user sessions **For Backend & Bot Developers:** - Create powerful automation tools and bots with comprehensive API coverage - Process real-time data streams using our optimized Firehose implementation - Build custom AT Protocol services and bridges to other platforms - Access Ozone moderation tools for administrative operations **For AT Protocol Enthusiasts:** - Work with all core AT Protocol primitives: DIDs, NSIDs, AT URIs, Lexicons, and XRPC - Contribute to the growing ecosystem of decentralized social applications - Access cutting-edge features as the protocol evolves **Proven in Production:** Used by popular applications like SkyFeed, deck.blue, SkyThrow, and many others serving thousands of users daily. Our SDK is actively maintained, thoroughly tested, and designed for the demands of production applications. ## 2. Package Ecosystem 📦 The atproto.dart ecosystem is organized into focused packages that work together seamlessly. Choose the packages that match your project's needs, from low-level AT Protocol primitives to high-level client libraries. ### 2.1. Core Libraries *Foundation packages for AT Protocol primitives and data structures* | Package | Description | pub.dev | Docs | | ------- | ----------- | :-----: | :--: | | **[at_primitives](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/at_primitives)** | AT Protocol primitive types (identifiers, URIs, NSIDs) - unified package | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/at_primitives.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/at_primitives) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/at_primitives/README.md) | | **[xrpc](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/xrpc)** | [XRPC](https://atproto.com/specs/xrpc) HTTP client with built-in retry and error handling | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/xrpc.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/xrpc) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/xrpc/README.md) | | **[multiformats](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/multiformats)** | IPFS multiformats support for content addressing (CIDs, multihash, etc.) | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/multiformats.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/multiformats) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/multiformats/README.md) | | **[lexicon](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/lexicon)** | [Lexicon](https://atproto.com/guides/lexicon) schema parsing and validation | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/lexicon.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/lexicon) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/lexicon/README.md) | | **[atproto_core](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto_core)** | Shared utilities and base functionality for AT Protocol clients | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/atproto_core.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/atproto_core) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto_core/README.md) | ### 2.2. Client Libraries *High-level API clients for AT Protocol services* | Package | Description | pub.dev | Docs | | ------- | ----------- | :-----: | :--: | | **[atproto](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto)** | Complete AT Protocol client (`com.atproto.*` endpoints) with Firehose support | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/atproto.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/atproto) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto/README.md) / [GUIDE](https://atprotodart.com/docs/packages/atproto) | | **[bluesky](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky)** | Full-featured Bluesky client (`app.bsky.*`, `chat.bsky.*` + AT Protocol) | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/bluesky.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/bluesky) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky/README.md) / [GUIDE](https://atprotodart.com/docs/packages/bluesky) | | **[atproto_oauth](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto_oauth)** | OAuth DPoP authentication client for secure user sessions | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/atproto_oauth.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/atproto_oauth) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/atproto_oauth/README.md) | | **[did_plc](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/did_plc)** | PLC Directory client for DID resolution and management | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/did_plc.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/did_plc) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/did_plc/README.md) | ### 2.3. Utilities & Tools *Specialized packages for text processing* | Package | Description | pub.dev | Docs | | ------- | ----------- | :-----: | :--: | | **[bluesky_text](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky_text)** | Rich text parsing for mentions, links, hashtags, and formatting | [![pub package](https://img.shields.io/pub/v/bluesky_text.svg?logo=dart&logoColor=00b9fc)](https://pub.dartlang.org/packages/bluesky_text) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky_text/README.md) / [GUIDE](https://atprotodart.com/docs/packages/bluesky_text) | ### 2.4. CLI Tool | Tool | Install | Docs | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------: | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: | | **[bluesky_cli](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky_cli)**: command line tool for `app.bsky.*` endpoints | `dart pub global activate bluesky_cli` | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/atproto.dart/tree/main/packages/bluesky_cli/README.md) | ### 2.5. GitHub Actions Workflow | Workflow | Marketplace | Docs | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------: | | **[bluesky-post](https://github.com/myConsciousness/bluesky-post)**: workflow for scheduled post to Bluesky from GitHub Actions | [![bluesky-post](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=actions&message=myConsciousness/bluesky-post&logo=GitHub&color=00acee)](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/send-bluesky-post) | [README](https://github.com/myConsciousness/bluesky-post/blob/main/README.md) | ## 3. Quick Start Guide 🚀 Get up and running with AT Protocol and Bluesky in minutes. Choose your use case below: ### 3.1. Building a Bluesky Social App Perfect for creating mobile apps, web clients, or social features. ```bash dart pub add bluesky ``` ```dart import 'package:bluesky/atproto.dart'; import 'package:bluesky/bluesky.dart'; void main() async { // Create authenticated session final session = await createSession( identifier: 'your-handle.bsky.social', password: 'your-password', ); final bsky = Bluesky.fromSession

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: **The comprehensive Dart/Flutter SDK for the AT Protocol ecosystem** - empowering developers to build the next generation of decentralized social applications with Bluesky and beyond.

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: The AT Protocol represents a paradigm shift toward decentralized social networking, and [Bluesky](https://blueskyweb.xyz) is leading this transformation. As the ecosystem grows rapidly with millions of users and thousands of developers building innovative applications, the need for robust, well-designed development tools has never been greater.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: **[atproto.dart](https://atprotodart.com)** is the most comprehensive and battle-tested SDK for AT Protocol development in Dart and Flutter. Whether you're building mobile apps, web applications, bots, or backend services, our SDK provides everything you need to integrate with the AT Protocol ecosystem efficiently and reliably.

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: **For Mobile & Web Developers:** - Build cross-platform social apps with Flutter using production-ready AT Protocol integration - Access the complete Bluesky API including posts, feeds, profiles, messaging, and moderation - Leverage built-in OAuth DPoP authentication for secure user sessions

scout.summary: The repository 'justoneapi/data-api' presents a commercial data API service offering scraping capabilities across numerous Chinese and international platforms, including e-commerce, social media, and job boards. It lacks source code or technical implementation details, functioning primarily as a promotional landing page.
highlights: Offers data APIs for over 30 platforms including Xiaohongshu, Taobao, Bilibili, Zhihu, Facebook, YouTube, and Twitter.
Repository contains only documentation (README) and logo assets—no actual code, scripts, or API implementation.
Clearly positioned as a commercial product with links to official website and API documentation.
risks: No open-source implementation provided; users cannot verify functionality, security, or compliance.
Scraping third-party platforms (e.g., Taobao, Xiaohongshu, Facebook) may violate their terms of service, posing legal and operational risks.
Absence of license file leaves usage rights ambiguous.

inventor.summary: The repository describes a commercial data API service that aggregates access to scraped data from over 30 platforms, but provides no actual implementation, source code, or technical innovation—only a marketing README and logos.
highlights: No novel invention or technical implementation is present; the repository serves as a promotional front for a proprietary API service.
Claims coverage of diverse platforms (e.g., Taobao, Xiaohongshu, Facebook, YouTube) but offers no evidence of unique data extraction methods or architectural design.
Lacks any code, algorithms, or system diagrams that would indicate inventive activity in web scraping, data normalization, or API orchestration.
risks: Absence of technical details prevents assessment of novelty, non-obviousness, or patentability of any underlying methods.
Reliance on third-party platform scraping may infringe intellectual property or terms of service, undermining defensibility of any claimed invention.
Without disclosed implementation, the repository cannot support claims of technical innovation or serve as prior art for genuine inventions.

engineer.summary: The repository 'justoneapi/data-api' functions as a marketing page for a commercial data API service, listing support for over 30 platforms including Taobao, Xiaohongshu, and international social media. It contains no source code, technical documentation, or implementation details—only a README and logo assets.
highlights: No actual code, scripts, or API implementation is present in the repository; only promotional content and platform listings are provided.
Supported platforms span e-commerce (Taobao, JD), social media (Xiaohongshu, Facebook, YouTube), job boards (Boss Zhipin), and travel (Ctrip), indicating broad but unverified scraping coverage.
Repository structure consists solely of README.md, image assets, and standard GitHub files—lacking any engineering artifacts like configs, tests, or deployment scripts.
risks: Absence of source code prevents verification of reliability, performance, security, or compliance with platform terms of service.
Heavy reliance on scraping third-party platforms may lead to legal exposure, IP blocking, or service instability due to anti-bot measures.
No versioning, release history, or issue tracking reduces trustworthiness and maintainability from an engineering standpoint.

skeptic.summary: The repository claims to offer a comprehensive data API service covering dozens of major platforms, yet provides no verifiable implementation, technical proof, or transparency—raising serious doubts about its legitimacy, reliability, and compliance.
highlights: Makes sweeping claims about supporting data extraction from over 30 high-profile platforms (e.g., Taobao, Xiaohongshu, Facebook, YouTube) without providing any code, samples, or technical validation.
Presents only a marketing README and logo files—no scripts, API specs, authentication examples, or rate limit disclosures that would allow independent verification.
Promotes access to platforms known for aggressive anti-scraping measures (e.g., Taobao, Xiaohongshu), yet offers zero evidence of sustainable or authorized data collection methods.
risks: High likelihood of overpromising: without source code or live examples, users cannot confirm whether the advertised endpoints actually work or are merely aspirational.
Potential bait-and-switch: the repo may exist solely to funnel users to a paid service with unclear deliverables, SLAs, or data provenance.
Legal exposure for users: if the service relies on unauthorized scraping, consumers of the API could inherit liability for violating platform terms of service.

scout.summary: The repository 'fast-vite-electron' is a Vite + Electron template optimized for speed using esbuild and custom tooling like Doubleshot and einf. It targets rapid development of Electron apps with Vue, offering IPC communication via TypeScript decorators and integrations with tsup and electron-builder. The author recommends alternative NestJS-based templates for production use.
highlights: Built on the author's custom framework 'einf', which is noted as potentially incomplete for production use [ID:E-RD-004].
Uses 'Doubleshot' tooling for fast Electron main process builds and development [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-003, E-RD-006, E-RD-008].
Features TypeScript decorator-based IPC communication between controllers/services [ID:E-RD-007].
Provides alternative production-ready templates using NestJS for Vue, React, and Svelte [ID:E-RD-005].
Repository includes standard configuration files and active dependency management (Renovate) [ID:E-RF-001, E-IS-001].
risks: Reliance on an author-maintained, possibly immature framework ('einf') may introduce stability or maintenance concerns in production [ID:E-RD-004].
Low number of forks (22) and modest stars (253) suggest limited community adoption or validation.
No official releases published, indicating the project may still be in early or experimental stages [repo_profile].

inventor.summary: The repository introduces a novel development stack combining Vite, Electron, and the author's custom tools—Doubleshot and einf—to enable rapid Electron app prototyping with TypeScript decorator-based IPC. While optimized for speed via esbuild, tsup, and electron-builder, it explicitly defers to NestJS-based alternatives for production use.
highlights: Invents a streamlined Electron + Vite workflow using the author’s Doubleshot toolkit for fast build/start cycles [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-003, E-RD-006, E-RD-008].
Introduces 'einf', a lightweight custom framework enabling TypeScript decorator-driven IPC between controllers and services in Electron [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-007].
Offers a modular template architecture, with this repo as a minimal base and separate NestJS-integrated variants for Vue, React, and Svelte [ID:E-RD-005].
Integrates modern tooling (tsup, electron-builder, esbuild) via @doubleshot/builder to accelerate development without Webpack [ID:E-RD-008].
risks: The core framework 'einf' is self-authored and acknowledged as potentially incomplete, posing architectural and maintenance risks for anything beyond prototyping [ID:E-RD-004].
Tight coupling to niche, author-maintained tools (Doubleshot, einf) may limit long-term sustainability and community support compared to standard Electron/Vite setups.
Absence of releases and low fork count suggest the invention remains experimental and unvalidated at scale [repo_profile].

engineer.summary: The repository provides a Vite + Electron template optimized for fast development using esbuild, tsup, and electron-builder, orchestrated through the author's custom Doubleshot toolkit and lightweight 'einf' framework. It enables TypeScript decorator-based IPC communication and serves as a minimal starting point, with explicit recommendations to use NestJS-integrated variants for production.
highlights: Leverages Doubleshot tooling—including vite-plugin-doubleshot and @doubleshot/builder—for rapid Electron main process builds and startup [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-003, E-RD-006, E-RD-008].
Implements IPC via TypeScript decorators using the author’s 'einf' framework, enabling structured controller/service communication [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-007].
Integrates modern build tools (tsup, electron-builder, esbuild) without Webpack, prioritizing speed and simplicity [ID:E-RD-008].
Clearly distinguishes this template as experimental, directing production users to alternative NestJS-based templates for Vue, React, and Svelte [ID:E-RD-005].
Includes standard project hygiene: ESLint config, TS configs, Renovate for dependency updates, and IDE support (.vscode) [ID:E-RF-001, E-IS-001].
risks: Dependence on 'einf', an author-maintained framework explicitly noted as potentially incomplete, introduces uncertainty for stability, testing, and long-term maintenance [ID:E-RD-004].
Tight coupling to niche, non-standard tooling (Doubleshot, einf) may hinder onboarding, debugging, and community support compared to mainstream Electron/Vite solutions.
No official releases and limited adoption (253 stars, 22 forks) suggest the stack is still experimental and lacks real-world validation at scale [repo_profile].

skeptic.summary: The repository presents an experimental Vite + Electron template built on author-specific tools (Doubleshot, einf) with explicit caveats against production use, raising concerns about maturity, maintainability, and reliance on non-standard abstractions.
highlights: The README explicitly states the underlying framework 'einf' may be incomplete and advises using NestJS-based alternatives for production [ID:E-RD-004].

Core functionality depends on niche, self-authored tooling (Doubleshot, vite-plugin-doubleshot, @doubleshot/builder) with limited external adoption or documentation [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-003, E-RD-006, E-RD-008].
Despite claims of speed and modern tooling, the project has no official releases and minimal community traction (253 stars, 22 forks), suggesting it remains unproven [repo_profile].
IPC communication relies on TypeScript decorators via 'einf', a pattern that adds abstraction without clear advantages over standard Electron IPC mechanisms [ID:E-RD-007].
risks: Using this template for anything beyond prototyping risks technical debt due to dependence on an immature, single-maintainer framework ('einf') [ID:E-RD-004].
The tight integration with Doubleshot ecosystem creates vendor lock-in, reducing flexibility and increasing maintenance burden if those tools stagnate [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-003, E-RD-006].
Lack of releases, sparse issue activity (only 2 open issues, 1 total), and absence of contribution guidelines signal low project maturity and support [repo_profile, E-IS-001].
Promotion of decorator-based IPC may obscure standard Electron patterns, complicating debugging and onboarding for developers unfamiliar with the custom abstraction [ID:E-RD-007].

scout.summary: The repository 'atproto.dart' is a well-structured, production-ready Dart/Flutter SDK for the AT Protocol and Bluesky ecosystem, featuring comprehensive tooling, clear documentation, and active maintenance indicators.
highlights: Comprehensive Dart/Flutter SDK targeting the AT Protocol and Bluesky Social with support for mobile, web, and backend services.
Rich documentation with clear sections covering package ecosystem, quick start guides, development setup, and contribution guidelines.
Includes core libraries, client libraries, utilities, CLI tools, and GitHub Actions workflows indicating mature project scaffolding.
Project displays strong community signals: 206 stars, 28 forks, CI badges, license, code of conduct, and sponsorship options.
risks: No official releases or version tags yet (releases_count: 0), which may complicate dependency management in production environments.
README is truncated in evidence, so completeness of critical documentation (e.g., API reference, migration guides) cannot be fully verified.

inventor.summary: atproto.dart is a comprehensive, production-ready Dart/Flutter SDK that enables developers to build decentralized social applications on the AT Protocol and Bluesky ecosystem, offering full API coverage, cross-platform support, and integrated tooling.
highlights: Provides complete access to Bluesky APIs including posts, feeds, profiles, messaging, and moderation [ID:E-RD-008].
Supports diverse development scenarios: mobile apps (Flutter), web, bots, and backend services [ID:E-RD-007].
Includes built-in OAuth DPoP authentication for secure user sessions [ID:E-RD-008].
Structured as a multi-package ecosystem with core libraries, clients, utilities, CLI, and CI workflows [ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RF-001].
Positioned as the most comprehensive and battle-tested AT Protocol SDK in Dart [ID:E-RD-007].
risks: Lack of official releases (releases_count: 0) may hinder stable dependency management despite production-ready claims.
Heavy reliance on external standards (AT Protocol, OAuth DPoP) without evidence of fallback mechanisms or offline capabilities.

engineer.summary: atproto.dart is a well-architected, multi-package Dart/Flutter SDK for the AT Protocol and Bluesky, featuring modular design, CI integration, and comprehensive API support—but lacks versioned releases, posing dependency risks.
highlights: Modular monorepo structure with distinct packages for core, client, utilities, and CLI components [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RD-004].
Integrated CI via GitHub Actions with test/analyzer workflows, indicating active quality assurance [ID:E-RD-003].
Full Bluesky API coverage including posts, feeds, profiles, messaging, and moderation with secure OAuth DPoP auth [ID:E-RD-008].
Supports cross-platform development (mobile, web, backend) using Flutter and Dart, aligned with production-ready claims [ID:E-RD-007].
risks: No tagged releases (releases_count: 0) despite production-ready positioning, complicating stable dependency resolution in downstream projects.
Truncated README prevents verification of critical engineering docs like error handling, rate limiting, or network resilience strategies.

skeptic.summary: While atproto.dart presents itself as a production-ready, comprehensive SDK for the AT Protocol and Bluesky in Dart/Flutter, several claims lack verifiable evidence or contradict observable project metrics, raising concerns about maturity and reliability.
highlights: The README repeatedly asserts the SDK is 'production-ready', 'battle-tested', and 'the most comprehensive' [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-007], yet there are zero official releases (releases_count: 0), suggesting these claims may be aspirational rather than factual.

Despite claiming support for 'backend services' and 'bots' [ID:E-RD-007][ID:E-RD-008], the repository structure and documentation focus heavily on Flutter/mobile use cases, with no evident backend-specific tooling or examples in the provided evidence.

The project promotes 'built-in OAuth DPoP authentication' [ID:E-RD-008], but no implementation details, security audits, or dependency versions are visible in the truncated README to substantiate robustness or compliance.
risks: Overstated maturity: Labeling the SDK as 'production-ready' and 'battle-tested' without versioned releases, changelogs, or adoption evidence from known projects [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-007] risks misleading developers into using unstable code in critical applications.

Documentation gaps: The README is truncated, and key technical assurances (e.g., error recovery, rate limit handling, token refresh logic) are absent from available excerpts, making it impossible to validate reliability claims.

Lack of release discipline: With 206 stars but no tagged releases [repo_profile.releases_count=0], dependency management becomes fragile—users must rely on volatile main branch commits, increasing integration risk.
